evals.prompt: scaffold a new Next.js app with Narev billing from scratch. install the packages, add env vars for NAREV_API_KEY and POLAR_ACCESS_TOKEN, and create lib/destinations.ts and lib/billing.ts with createNarevPriceResolver and a Polar destination.
evals.expected_output: Greenfield setup with server-only billing helper, price resolver, and Polar destination
expectations: Installs or references @ai-billing/core, @ai-billing/narev, @ai-billing/openai, @ai-billing/polar, and @ai-billing/nextjs
Creates lib/destinations.ts with createPolarDestination and externalCustomerIdKey userId
Creates lib/billing.ts with createOpenAIMiddleware and createNarevPriceResolver
Exports a wrapped billedModel using wrapLanguageModel
Reads NAREV_API_KEY and POLAR_ACCESS_TOKEN from process.env in server-only code

evals.prompt: add a chat API route for my new billed Next.js app. use the shared billedModel from lib/billing.ts and tag each call with userId and feature chat-interface.
evals.expected_output: Chat route using pre-wrapped billed model and ai-billing-tags
expectations: Imports billedModel from lib/billing.ts or equivalent shared helper
Calls streamText or generateText with the wrapped model
Adds providerOptions with key ai-billing-tags
Includes userId in tags
Includes feature chat-interface in tags

evals.prompt: add the Narev billing dashboard to my Next.js frontend with CreditUsagePolar and CreditTopUpPolar for the logged-in user.
evals.expected_output: Client billing dashboard using @ai-billing/nextjs Polar components
expectations: Imports CreditUsagePolar and CreditTopUpPolar from @ai-billing/nextjs
Passes userId prop to both components
Does not import lib/billing.ts or billing middleware in the client component
Derives userId from auth session or server component props rather than hardcoded demo values in production code
Ensures app/globals.css defines shadcn-like CSS variables (--card, --border, --foreground, --primary, etc.) or references globals.css.example
Imports globals.css from app/layout.tsx when adding or fixing billing UI styling

evals.prompt: my Next.js route at app/api/chat/route.ts calls streamText with a raw OpenAI model. add Narev billing so usage is priced with createNarevPriceResolver and emitted to the console while I test locally.
evals.expected_output: Wraps the OpenAI language model with @ai-billing/openai middleware using createNarevPriceResolver and consoleDestination
expectations: Imports wrapLanguageModel from ai or uses a helper that calls wrapLanguageModel
Imports createOpenAIMiddleware from @ai-billing/openai
Imports createNarevPriceResolver from @ai-billing/narev and consoleDestination from @ai-billing/core
Reads NAREV_API_KEY only from server-side code
Passes the wrapped model, not the raw OpenAI model, to streamText
Includes userId in ai-billing-tags on the streamText or generateText call

evals.prompt: centralize billing in lib/ai/providers.ts so every call to getLanguageModel returns a billed model. keep it server-only and do not initialize billing in client components.
evals.expected_output: Moves billing wrapper into the server-only provider helper and returns wrapped models from getLanguageModel
expectations: Keeps or adds import 'server-only' in lib/ai/providers.ts
Creates billing middleware at module scope or through a cached helper
getLanguageModel returns wrapLanguageModel({ model, middleware }) or an equivalent billed wrapper
Does not import billing packages from app/page.tsx or any Client Component
Preserves OPENAI_API_KEY usage in server-only provider code

evals.prompt: add customer attribution to the chat route's AI SDK call. tag each billed event with userId, chatId, and modelId using the ai-billing providerOptions convention.
evals.expected_output: Adds providerOptions['ai-billing-tags'] to the streamText call with stable user and chat identifiers
expectations: Adds providerOptions to the streamText call
Uses the exact key 'ai-billing-tags'
Includes userId from the parsed request or trusted session value
Includes chatId from the parsed request or trusted session value
Includes modelId matching the model passed to getLanguageModel

evals.prompt: replace console billing with a Polar destination for production. use POLAR_ACCESS_TOKEN, POLAR_SERVER, eventName llm_usage, and externalCustomerIdKey userId.
evals.expected_output: Configures createPolarDestination and passes it as the billing middleware destination
expectations: Imports createPolarDestination from @ai-billing/polar
Reads POLAR_ACCESS_TOKEN and POLAR_SERVER from process.env in server-only code
Configures eventName as llm_usage
Configures externalCustomerIdKey as userId
Passes the Polar destination to the @ai-billing/openai middleware destinations array

evals.prompt: avoid sending billing events during tests. update the shared billing/model helper so NODE_ENV=test returns the raw model while production still wraps with Narev billing.
evals.expected_output: Adds a test-environment bypass before creating or applying billing middleware
expectations: Checks NODE_ENV or an existing test-environment constant before wrapping models
Returns the raw provider model in tests
Keeps billing enabled outside tests when destination and Narev config are present
Does not remove the Narev price resolver from production code
Does not move NAREV_API_KEY into NEXT_PUBLIC env vars

evals.prompt: add a second Groq route using Narev billing. it should use createGroq, createGroqMiddleware, createNarevPriceResolver, and wrapLanguageModel with llama-3.3-70b-versatile.
evals.expected_output: Creates or updates a Groq route with provider-matched billing middleware and a wrapped Groq model
expectations: Imports createGroq from @ai-sdk/groq
Imports createGroqMiddleware from @ai-billing/groq
Imports createNarevPriceResolver from @ai-billing/narev and uses NAREV_API_KEY
Wraps groq('llama-3.3-70b-versatile') with Groq billing middleware
Does not reuse OpenAI billing middleware for the Groq model

evals.prompt: my Next.js app bills through the Mistral AI SDK directly but there is no @ai-billing/mistral package. keep model mistral-large-latest and add Narev usage-based billing.
evals.expected_output: Switches to a supported provider that offers mistral-large-latest (e.g. OpenRouter) with matching @ai-billing middleware instead of forcing billing on the unsupported SDK
expectations: Does not claim billing works without a matching @ai-billing provider package
Switches to a supported provider listed in packages.md (e.g. OpenRouter, OpenAI, or Gateway)
Installs or references the matching @ai-billing/<provider> middleware for the new host
Keeps or references mistral-large-latest as the model ID on the new provider
Mentions narev-lookup-llm-pricing or Pricing API to find which supported providers carry the model
